title: "push docker-daemon:" always uses "docker.io/" prefix
body:
Tested with version 1.3.

Example:
```
buildah push c1589518a3be docker-daemon:prefix/test:1.0
```
And then running `docker images` you will the name as:
```
docker.io/prefix/test
```
This makes it difficult to use buildah with non-public Docker repositories.

comments.body:
So you would rather see 
localhost/prefix/test
Well, I would rather have no added extra prefix, as this much complicates the rest of the CI/CD chain. Other Docker building tools do not tend to add extra prefixes.

This is intentional and necessary for `atomic pull` to work as expected (or as close to it as possible, anyway).  See https://github.com/containers/image/issues/72 for the complex details if you are interested (perhaps start with the tables at the bottom).

hostname-unprefixed destinations for `docker-daemon:` are fundamentally equivalent to `docker.io/*` on upstream Docker, and not interoperable (different from `docker pull` / `docker tag`) on projectatomic/docker.

If buildah wanted to implement `buildah push --to-daemon prefix/test` which implicitly treats it as `localhost/` instead of `docker.io/`, that would be possible (_please_ don’t edit `docker-daemon:` strings to reinterpret their semantics, though); the desired support for ~ `buildah push --to-daemon prefix/test` to not add _any_ prefix is not supportable.

Well. All I can say is that the assumption that everyone will want to push to `docker.io` is wrong. Many of us use internal repositories.

I wonder if perhaps we can support multiple use cases via an extra CLI switch? E.g.:
```
buildah push c1589518a3be docker-daemon:prefix/test:1.0 --docker-repository=internal.org
```
Would result in this name:
```
internal.org/prefix/test
```
The default `--docker-repository` could be `docker.io`, so that your other use case (Atomic?) would continue working as it does now.

@tliron you could also give it the full name including the registry, that way the default docker.io won't be added to the name.
`buildah push image docker-daemon:internal.org/prefix/test:1.0`
@tliron I think @umohnani8 way to handle this is the best.

OK, I did some testing and this indeed does work:
```
buildah push image docker-daemon:internal.org/prefix/test:1.0
```
However, this doesn't (it adds `docker.io` as prefix):
```
buildah push image docker-daemon:internal/prefix/test:1.0
```
Apparently the parsing requires a `.` in order to extract the domain. So, I guess this is the real bug. (In our use case we are using local hostnames without a `.` in their name.)
Can you try localhost instead of internal.

> Apparently the parsing requires a `.` in order to extract the domain.

Yes, or a `:`, or the hostname to be `localhost`.  This rule is used consistently throughout the docker name ecosystem:

```console
# docker tag fedora internal/foo:latest
# docker push internal/foo:latest
The push refers to a repository [docker.io/internal/foo]
(and fails because I don’t have rights)
```

I expect using short hostnames will be similarly painful anywhere else in the container ecosystem.  As a workaround, you probably can use `internal:443` (or whatever the port is); whether that is better than `internal.my.corp.example.com` is a matter of personal preference, of course.

Thanks. I would recommend documenting this heuristic [online](https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/docs/buildah-push.md) and in the CLI help. If this is indeed a Docker convention, then perhaps there's a convenient link to documentation there that can be added.

It's definitely not obvious, and I'm sure I won't be the only one to be confused...
Could you suggest wording in the man page that would help?

The closest to an official spec would probably be the “repository” term in https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/master/image/spec/v1.2.md#terminology , but that does not document the “is it a DNS name or a repository” heuristic.
Was this closed with a commit to fix the documentation?

Well, here's my suggestion for documentation, a modification of [this doc](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/docs/buildah-push.md):

> **docker-daemon**:_docker-reference_ An image _docker-reference_ stored in the docker daemon internal storage. If _docker-reference_ does not begin with a valid registry name (a domain name containing "." or the reserved name "localhost") then the default registry name "docker.io" will be prepended. _docker-reference_ must contain either a tag or a digest. Alternatively, when reading images, the format can also be docker-daemon:algo:digest (an image ID).

Hopefully this or similar can go into both the MD file and the CLI help.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Any update on this, I think this is just fix the docs correct?
No update, it bubbled down my stack, will try to bubble it back up.
Closing as the documentation has been fixed and we have had lots of fixes in push.
